Resources | Approaches

Putting lessons into practice

We learn how to be a respectful philanthropic partner by listening, building relationships, and making grants to leaders across Washington. These tools guide our work. We share them to strengthen our collective efforts toward equity and racial justice.

Living language guide

The Living Language Guide serves as a resource for Inatai Foundation team and board members to establish a shared understanding of the language we use for our work together. It is also a resource for partners to gain a deeper understanding of our values and worldview.

Download the Guide

APPROACH: BUILDING RELATIONSHIPS WITH COMMUNITIES

We know that building a statewide foundation that is both reflective of and responsive to communities will only happen through deep, sustained relationships.

Download the document

Approach: Information, learning, and evaluation

We ground our learning in community wisdom and are dedicated to sharing what we learn from our successes and setbacks.

Download the document

A commitment to building community power

Inatai Foundation is a 501(c)(4) philanthropy seeking to transform the balance of power to ensure equity and racial justice across Washington state and beyond. We are accountable to leaders and organizations building power in racially diverse communities in our state. With a team based throughout Washington, we work to advance four distinct areas of work: grantmaking, relationship building, policy and advocacy, and investment management.
